codeReview.summary: The rerun adds a `ProviderInstanceBase` alias and focused provider-instance unit suites, but it still does not make the ProviderConnectionConfig-derived base authoritative for `ProviderInstance`. The same blocker from `shared-consolidate-profile-types` remains, and the commit also reintroduces out-of-scope version bumps.

issues.description: The original shared-base-type blocker is still not fully resolved. `ProviderInstance` now extends `Partial<ProviderInstanceBase>`, but it still keeps a separate required `providerType` field and `extractConnectionFromProvider()` still derives the exported `provider` value from `providerType` at line 85 instead of from the shared base field. That leaves two provider identifiers on the same object and allows them to diverge, so generic code still cannot treat `ProfileConfig` and `ProviderInstance` as the same shared ProviderConnectionConfig-derived shape. The new tests preserve this translation behavior rather than closing the gap (`tests/unit/suites/provider-instance/base-type.test.ts:155-169`, `tests/unit/suites/provider-instance/features.test.ts:36-37`).

Commit `4fea6c4` again bundles unrelated version bumps in `package.json`, `packages/extension/manifest.json`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json` even though this feature only targets the provider-instance type fix. Mission AGENTS says non-release refactor features must not include those files (`/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/AGENTS.md:214-219`), and `git show --stat 4fea6c4` confirms all three version files changed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared-library architecture note is still stale for provider types. It still points workers to `packages/shared/src/providers.ts`, while the repo now uses `profile.ts`, `connection-config.ts`, and `provider-instance.ts` for this area.

summary: Review failed. The fix adds a ProviderConnectionConfig-based alias and new unit coverage, but `ProviderInstance` still relies on legacy `providerType` as the canonical provider field, so the earlier shared-base-type blocker remains. The commit also reintroduces forbidden package/manifest version bumps outside the feature scope.
